info.title: devops
info.description: ﻿devops is a command line tool designed to interact with the Azure DevOps and Azure DevOps Server APIs

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: CommandLineApplication.Parse, OK: CommandLineApplication.Execute, OK: CommandLineApplication.ExecuteAsync, OK: CommandLineApplication.ctor(), OK: CommandLineApplication.ctor(IConsole), OK: CommandLineApplication.ctor(IConsole, String), OK: CommandLineApplication.ctor(IHelpTextGenerator, IConsole, String))

options.name: --version
options.description: Gets the version of the devops cli tool.

options.name: --service-url
options.description: URL to the service you will connect to, e.g. https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

options.name: --build-definition-id
options.description: Release definition id to filter the resutl

options.name: --top
options.description: Number of releases to get. Default is 50.

options.name: --service-url
options.description: URL to the service you will connect to, e.g. https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

options.description: URL to the service you will connect to, e.g. https://dev.azure.com/organization.

commands.name: list
commands.description: Get a list of build definitions.

options.description: URL to the service you will connect to, e.g. https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

options.name: --output-file

options.description: URL to the service you will connect to, e.g. https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

options.name: --build-definition-id
options.description: Build definition id. if this value is not provided the import command will create a new build definition group otherwise it will attempt to update the build definition with the provided identifier.

options.name: --input-file

options.description: URL to the service you will connect to, e.g. https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

commands.name: pull-request
commands.description: Retrieve git pull requests for a project

options.name: --service-url
options.description: URL to the service you will connect to, e.g. https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

commands.name: list
commands.description: Get a list of pull requests in a git repository.

options.name: --repository
options.description: The id or friendly name of the repository.

options.name: --top
options.description: Number of pull requests to get.

options.name: --skip
options.description: Number of pull requests to skip.

options.name: --timerange-type
options.description: The type of time range on which the pull requests are filtered.

options.name: --min-time
options.description: If specified, filters pull requests that created/closed after this date based on the queryTimeRangeType specified.

options.name: --max-time
options.description: If specified, filters pull requests that created/closed before this date based on the queryTimeRangeType specified.

options.name: --status
options.description: The status of the pull request.

options.name: --export-all
options.description: If specified, retrieves and exports all available pull requests.

options.name: --output-file
options.description: File to export the pullrequest list. If this value is not provided the output will be the console. If --export-all is set this --output-file is required.

options.name: --service-url
options.description: URL to the service you will connect to, e.g. https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

options.name: --release-definition-id
options.description: Release definition id to filter the resutl

options.name: --definition-environment-id
options.description: Environment id value within the release definition to filter the resutl

options.name: --top
options.description: Number of releases to get. Default is 50.

options.name: --expand-property
options.description: The property that should be expanded in the list of releases. More info here https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/azure/devops/release/releases/list?view=azure-devops-server-rest-5.0#releaseexpands

options.name: --environment-status-filter
options.description: Set the environemnt status to filter the output list. More info here https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/azure/devops/release/releases/get%20release%20environment?view=azure-devops-rest-5.1#environmentstatus

options.name: --all-properties
options.description: If present each item in the output list of releases will contain all the release properties

options.name: --service-url
options.description: URL to the service you will connect to, e.g. https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

commands.name: create
commands.description: Request (create) a release given a release definition.

options.name: --artifacts
options.description: Json string that represent the list of ArtifactsMetadata that will be use in the release(i.e [{'Name': 'artifact-name', 'Alias': 'artifact-alias', 'Id': 'artifact-id'}])

commands.name: list
commands.description: Get a list of release approvals.

options.name: --status
options.description: Filter approvals with this status. Default is 'pending'. (valid values here https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/azure/devops/release/approvals/list?view=azure-devops-rest-5.1#approvalstatus)

options.name: --release-id
options.description: Release id. You can specify this multiple times to include multiple releases in the filter

options.name: --output-file
options.description: File to export the approval details. If this value is not provided the output will be the console.

options.description: URL to the service you will connect to, e.g. https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

options.name: --approval-id
options.description: Id of the approval to update

options.name: --status
options.description: Set the approval status. (valid values here https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/azure/devops/release/approvals/list?view=azure-devops-rest-5.1#approvalstatus)

commands.name: identity
commands.description: Resolve legacy identity information for use with older APIs such as the Security APIs.

options.name: --service-url
options.description: URL to the service you will connect to, e.g. https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

commands.name: list
commands.description: Resolve legacy identity information for use with older APIs such as the Security APIs.

options.name: --identity-ids
options.description: Specify a non-default scope (collection, project) to search for groups.

options.name: --query-membership
options.description: The membership information to include with the identities. Values can be None for no membership data or Direct to include the groups that the identity is a member of and the identities that are a member of this identity (groups only).

options.name: --search-filter
options.description: The type of search to perform. Values can be AccountName (domainlias), DisplayName, MailAddress, General (display name, account name, or unique name), or LocalGroupName (only search Azure Devops groups).

options.name: --filter-value
options.description: The search value, as specified by the searchFilter.
